name: Somni
notes: Reopened Nov 2024. Original Somni at SLS Beverly Hills operated 2018-2020 via ThinkFoodGroup.

name: Kato
notes: Originally opened in parents' former strip-mall space in Sawtelle. Relocated to Row DTLA 2022.

name: Vespertine
notes: Housed in Eric Owen Moss's 'Waffle' building. Closed during pandemic, reopened April 2024 as Vespertine 2.0.

name: Bestia
notes: Sprout LA (Bill Chait) involved in early development.

other: Bon Appetit #1 Best New Restaurant in America 2018

name: Campanile
notes: Co-opened by Nancy Silverton and Mark Peel. Operated for ~23 years. Defining LA restaurant.

name: La Brea Bakery
notes: Launched the artisan bread revolution in American retail.

name: Hatchet Hall
notes: Dunsmoor departed 2021. Wes Whitsell took over and earned Michelin star.

name: 88 Club
notes: Partnership between Mei Lin and restaurateur Francis Miranda.

name: Nightshade
notes: Closed during pandemic. Famous for mapo tofu lasagna.

name: Morihiro
notes: Reopened at Elysian location Oct 2025. Previous incarnation was Mori Sushi in West LA (2008-2011, 1 star).

name: Kismet
notes: Originally backed by Jon Shook & Vinny Dotolo. Split and became fully independent May 2021.

name: Gwen
notes: Includes butcher shop component.

name: Mori Nozomi
notes: In former Mori Sushi space. No relation to previous tenant.

name: The French Laundry
notes: Training ground referenced in LA chef lineages. Thomas Keller.

name: Alinea
notes: Training ground referenced in LA chef lineages. Grant Achatz.

name: elBulli
notes: Ferran Adria. Training ground for Aitor Zabala.

name: Blue Hill at Stone Barns
notes: Dan Barber. Training ground for Kramer and Hymanson.

name: Angelini Osteria
notes: Gino Angelini. Training ground for Ori Menashe.

name: Red Medicine
notes: Jordan Kahn's first LA restaurant.

name: Dialogue
notes: Dave Beran's first LA restaurant. Closed during COVID.

name: Kinn
notes: Ki Kim's first restaurant. Closed before he joined Meteora.

name: Spago
notes: Wolfgang Puck. Training ground for Nancy Silverton (pastry chef) and Michael Cimarusti (chef de cuisine).

name: Matsuhisa
notes: Nobu Matsuhisa. Training ground for Morihiro Onodera.

name: Damian
notes: Enrique Olvera. Training ground for Fatima Juarez.

name: Moto Azabu
notes: Training ground for Nozomi Mori under Takamichi Arai.

name: Jungsik
notes: Training ground for Ki Kim.

name: Atomix
notes: Training ground for Ki Kim.

name: Le Cirque
notes: Sirio Maccioni. Training ground for Daniel Boulud, Michael Cimarusti.

name: Water Grill
notes: Cimarusti was executive chef 1997-2004.

name: L'Arpège
notes: Alain Passard. Training ground for Michael Cimarusti.

name: Next
notes: Alinea Group. Dave Beran developed 15 menus as executive chef.

name: Per Se
notes: Thomas Keller. Training ground for Jordan Kahn.

name: Benu
notes: Corey Lee. Training ground for Jon Yao.

name: Coi
notes: Daniel Patterson. Training ground for Jon Yao.

name: Takao
notes: Training ground for Niki Nakayama under Morihiro Onodera.

name: Azami
notes: Niki Nakayama's pre-n/naka restaurant. Operated for 8 years.

name: Arzak
notes: Juan Mari Arzak. Training ground for Aitor Zabala.

name: Akelarre
notes: Pedro Subijana. Training ground for Aitor Zabala.

name: Ishikawa
notes: Hideki Ishikawa. Training ground for Brandon Hayato Go.

name: Ink
notes: Michael Voltaggio. Training ground for Mei Lin.

name: Michael's
notes: Michael McCarty. Jonathan Waxman was original chef. Training ground for Nancy Silverton. Birthplace of California cuisine alongside Chez Panisse.

name: Bouchon (Beverly Hills)
notes: Thomas Keller Restaurant Group. Training ground for Charles Namba.

name: Chanterelle
notes: David Waltuck. Training ground for Charles Namba.

name: Glasserie
notes: Sara Kramer was opening chef. Andrew Tarlow project.

name: Maude
notes: Curtis Stone. Single-ingredient seasonal menus. Converted to The Pie Room Sep 2024.

name: Marco Pierre White (London)
notes: Youngest chef to earn 3 Michelin stars (1995). Training ground for Curtis Stone, Gordon Ramsay. Aggregate entry for MPW's London restaurants (Harvey's, The Restaurant, Mirabelle).

name: Mori Sushi
notes: Morihiro Onodera. Predecessor to current Morihiro.

name: CUT
notes: Wolfgang Puck. At the Beverly Wilshire.

name: Chinois on Main
notes: Wolfgang Puck. Pioneered Asian-French fusion in America.

name: Ma Maison
notes: Patrick Terrail. Where Wolfgang Puck became famous before opening Spago.

name: République
notes: Walter & Margarita Manzke. In the former Campanile building on La Brea.

name: Bicyclette
notes: Walter & Margarita Manzke.

name: Sari Sari Store
notes: Margarita Manzke. Filipino bakery and café adjacent to République.

name: Felix Trattoria
notes: Evan Funke. Handmade pasta focus.

name: Mother Wolf
notes: Evan Funke. Roman-inspired. At the Hollywood Roosevelt.

name: Mélisse
notes: Josiah Citrin. Longest-running multi-starred restaurant in LA.

name: Charcoal
notes: Josiah Citrin.

name: Petit Trois
notes: Ludo Lefebvre. No-reservation French counter. Multiple LA locations.

name: Trois Mec
notes: Ludo Lefebvre, Jon Shook, Vinny Dotolo. Ticket-only tasting menu.

name: Otium
notes: Tim Hollingsworth. Adjacent to The Broad museum.

name: Native
notes: Nyesha Arrington.

name: Animal
notes: Jon Shook & Vinny Dotolo. Changed the LA dining landscape.

name: Jon & Vinny's
notes: Jon Shook & Vinny Dotolo. Multiple LA locations.

name: Son of a Gun
notes: Jon Shook & Vinny Dotolo.

name: Lucques
notes: Suzanne Goin & Caroline Styne.

name: a.o.c.
notes: Suzanne Goin & Caroline Styne. Wine-centric.

name: Tavern
notes: Suzanne Goin & Caroline Styne.

name: Rustic Canyon
notes: Jeremy Fox. Rustic Canyon Family.

name: Birdie G's
notes: Jeremy Fox. Jewish-American comfort food.

name: Kali
notes: Kevin Meehan.

name: Ditroit
notes: Enrique Olvera. Casual Mexican alongside Damian.

name: Patina
notes: Joachim Splichal. Moved to Walt Disney Concert Hall 2003. Training ground for Walter Manzke, Josiah Citrin.

name: L'Orangerie
notes: Major LA French fine dining institution for 28 years. Training ground for Walter Manzke, Ludo Lefebvre.

name: The Bazaar by José Andrés
notes: José Andrés. At the SLS Hotel.

name: Daniel
notes: Daniel Boulud. Training ground for Walter Manzke.

name: Chez Panisse
notes: Alice Waters. Birthplace of California cuisine. Training ground for Suzanne Goin.

name: Pujol
notes: Enrique Olvera. Flagship restaurant.

name: Five & Ten
notes: Hugh Acheson. Training ground for Brian Dunsmoor.

name: The Restaurant at Meadowood
notes: Destroyed in 2020 Glass Fire. Christopher Kostow, chef.

name: Sushi Ginza Onodera
notes: Nozomi Mori trained here.
